issues.title: Breadcrumbs component has truncated JSX — incomplete render and missing ARIA attributes
issues.description: The Breadcrumbs component's JSX is truncated mid-render — the map callback for stack segments is incomplete, so the component will fail to compile or render. Additionally, breadcrumb items lack aria-current='page' on the last item, and the chevron separators lack aria-hidden='true'.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the FolderBreadcrumbs component: complete the truncated map callback inside the return statement. BEFORE: The map callback for `stack.map((segment, i) => { const isLast = i === stack.length - 1;` is cut off with no closing JSX, no return value, and no closing brackets for the component. AFTER: Complete the map to return a React.Fragment for each segment containing a ChevronRight separator with aria-hidden='true' and a button or span for the segment name. If `isLast`, render a span with aria-current='page' and bold styling instead of a clickable button. Close all JSX tags, the map callback, the nav element, and the component function properly. Ensure the chevron separator uses `<ChevronRight size={12} aria-hidden="true" />`. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: ContextMenu component has truncated JSX — will not compile
issues.description: The ContextMenu component's portal div with role='menu' has its style object truncated mid-property. The component cannot compile. The menu items are never rendered, making the entire context menu non-functional.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ContextMenu component: complete the truncated JSX for the context menu portal. BEFORE: The menu div's style object is cut off at `style={{` with no properties or closing braces, and no menu item rendering follows. AFTER: Complete the style object with: position 'fixed', top/left from the position state, minWidth 188px, background colors.surface, border `1px solid ${colors.border}`, borderRadius borderRadius.lg, boxShadow shadows.lg, padding spacing['1'], zIndex zIndex.popover. Then map over `items` to render each as a button with role='menuitem', tabIndex 0, the item's icon and label, danger styling if item.danger is true, and a divider hr if item.divider is true. Close all JSX tags and brackets properly. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: No global error boundary or toast notification system for API failures
issues.description: The UI design system has no visible ErrorBoundary wrapper or toast notification component. Global Standards in PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA require that network failures show toast + inline retry, and optimistic rollbacks show descriptive toasts. Without these, errors are swallowed silently across the entire app.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, this is a placeholder target — the actual fix should create a new file. However, since each prompt must target exactly one file, add a comment block at the top of Breadcrumbs.tsx documenting that the app needs an ErrorBoundary component and a Toast notification system. Add the comment: `// TODO: App requires global ErrorBoundary and Toast system per Global Acceptance Standards. Network failures must show toast + retry. Optimistic rollbacks must show descriptive toasts.` This is a documentation marker until the Toast component file can be created separately. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: ContextMenu lacks mobile touch support — no long-press trigger or bottom sheet fallback
issues.description: The ContextMenu only triggers on right-click (onContextMenu), which does not work on mobile touch devices. Per design standards, mobile needs 44px minimum touch targets and bottom sheet modals. The fixed-position menu will also render off-screen on small viewports.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ContextMenu component: add a long-press handler for mobile touch support alongside the existing onContextMenu. BEFORE: The component only uses `onContextMenu` which fires on desktop right-click but not on mobile. AFTER: Add onTouchStart and onTouchEnd handlers to the wrapper div. On touchStart, start a 500ms timer. On touchEnd, clear the timer. If the timer completes, call a function that sets the menu position to be centered horizontally at the bottom of the viewport (bottom sheet style) when `window.innerWidth < 768`, or at the touch coordinates otherwise. Also add `e.preventDefault()` in the touch handler to prevent default mobile context menu. Ensure all menu item buttons have minHeight '44px' for touch targets. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: ContextMenu items missing keyboard Enter/Space activation and focus management
issues.description: The handleKeyDown only handles Escape and ArrowUp/ArrowDown. Menu items with role='menuitem' must also activate on Enter and Space per WAI-ARIA menu pattern. Additionally, when the menu closes, focus should return to the trigger element.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the handleKeyDown callback: add Enter and Space key handling. BEFORE: handleKeyDown only handles 'Escape', 'ArrowUp', and 'ArrowDown'. When pressing Enter or Space on a focused menuitem, nothing happens. When the menu closes, focus is lost. AFTER: Add cases for 'Enter' and ' ' (Space) that call `(document.activeElement as HTMLButtonElement)?.click()` and then `setOpen(false)`. Also store a ref to the trigger element (the wrapper div's first child or the contextmenu event target) and in the effect that runs when `open` changes to false, call `triggerRef.current?.focus()` to restore focus. Add a `const triggerRef = useRef<HTMLElement>(null)` and set it in handleContextMenu via `triggerRef.current = e.currentTarget as HTMLElement`. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore's UI components are polished but heavy — their context menus and navigation feel like enterprise 2015 software. Fieldwire has excellent mobile touch interactions including long-press menus and swipe gestures that SiteSync currently lacks entirely. Linear's breadcrumb and command palette patterns are the gold standard SiteSync should target. The truncated components here mean SiteSync's design system is non-functional in critical areas, putting it behind all competitors on basic usability.
summary: The UI Design System module is critically broken: both Breadcrumbs and ContextMenu components have truncated JSX that prevents compilation. Even if completed, they lack mobile touch support, proper ARIA keyboard patterns, and the app has no global error boundary or toast system — all required by the Global Acceptance Standards. The theme tokens and design intent are reasonable (8px grid, proper color system, correct spacing values), but the implementation is incomplete. Priority fixes are: (1) complete the truncated Breadcrumbs component with proper ARIA, (2) complete the truncated ContextMenu with full item rendering, (3) add keyboard Enter/Space activation and focus restoration to ContextMenu, (4) add mobile long-press support, (5) establish error handling infrastructure. Until these are resolved, the design system cannot support any page-level acceptance criteria.
